title: Pyramid of Bricks

content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN bricks and want to construct a **pyramid**. A pyramid is built in layers where the iii-th layer (from the top) requires **i** bricks.


The **1st** layer requires 111 brick.
The **2nd** layer requires 222 bricks.
The **3rd** layer requires 333 bricks.
And so on.
For example, following is a pyramid of 444 layers:

Your task is to determine the maximum number of **complete layers** you can build using the given NNN bricks.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains the number of test cases, TTT.
For each test case, a single integer, NNN, represents the number of bricks available.


Output Format

A single integer representing the maximum number of complete layers you can build.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N, the number of bricks available.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the maximum number of complete layers that can be built – on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=8 the largest k with k(k+1)/2 ≤ 8 is k=3 (3*4/2=6). For N=15 the sum of first 5 layers is exactly 15, so k=5.
N=1 → only layer 1 fits. N=2 → still only layer 1 fits because 1+2=3 exceeds 2. N=100 → k=13 because 13*14/2=91 ≤ 100 and 14*15/2=105 > 100.
Using the formula k = floor((sqrt(8*10^9+1)-1)/2) gives 44720. The sum of first 44720 layers is 999961560 which is ≤ 1e9, while adding the 44721‑st layer would exceed the limit.

solution.approach: The total bricks needed for k complete layers is the k‑th triangular number S = k*(k+1)/2. We need the largest k such that S ≤ N. This inequality can be transformed to k² + k - 2N ≤ 0. Solving the quadratic gives k = floor((sqrt(8N+1)-1)/2). Because of possible floating‑point rounding, we adjust k by at most one step using integer checks. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) overall.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){long N=Long.parseLong(br.readLine().trim());long k=(long)((Math.sqrt(8.0*N+1)-1)/2);while((k+1)*(k+2)/2<=N)k++;while(k*(k+1)/2>N)k--;sb.append(k).append("\n");}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
